description: Rust baseline invariants — three tight, security-focused rules only. Deliberately excludes .unwrap()/bare unsafe blocks: those are style/robustness concerns with far too high a false-positive rate to gate on. Covered here: spawning a shell with `-c` (command injection surface), mem::transmute (type-confusion / UB), and SQL assembled with format! into a query call (SQL injection).

rules.//: std::process::Command::new("sh"|"bash") followed by .arg("-c") hands a command line to a shell — the injection surface the argv API exists to avoid. Requiring the `.arg("-c")` (within the 5-line forward window via [\s\S]*?, so the match still STARTS on the Command::new line) keeps this from firing on benign `Command::new("sh")` uses that pass a fixed script path. Fix: call the target binary directly with separate .arg() values and no shell.
rules.message: Command::new("sh"/"bash").arg("-c", ...) runs the argument through a shell — any interpolated value becomes command injection. Invoke the target program directly with discrete .arg() values (no shell, no -c).

rules.//: mem::transmute reinterprets bytes between arbitrary types with no checks — a frequent source of undefined behavior (invalid enum/bool/pointer values, lifetime laundering). Matches std::mem::transmute, mem::transmute, and a bare transmute call/turbofish; the leading \b plus the explicit prefixes prevent matching inside identifiers like `my_transmute`. WARN because rare low-level code has a justified, audited use. Prefer safe conversions: `as`, From/TryFrom, to_ne_bytes/from_ne_bytes, or bytemuck/zerocopy for POD reinterpretation.
rules.message: mem::transmute reinterprets raw bytes with no validation and is a common source of undefined behavior. Use a checked conversion (as, TryFrom, from_ne_bytes) or bytemuck/zerocopy. If a transmute is genuinely required, isolate it in a small, commented, audited unsafe block.

rules.//: format! used to build a SQL string passed to query()/query_as() interpolates values straight into SQL — SQL injection, and it bypasses the compile-time checking of sqlx::query!/query_as!. Matches `query(&format!(` and `query_as(&format!(` (optional & for the borrow). The leading \b keeps it off identifiers like `subquery`. WARN because format! into a query is occasionally used for trusted, static fragments (sort direction, validated table name); confirm no user data reaches it. Fix: use bound parameters (`.bind(x)` with ? / $1 placeholders) or the compile-time-checked query! macros.
rules.message: format! building a SQL string passed to query()/query_as() interpolates values into SQL — a SQL injection sink that also defeats sqlx's compile-time checks. Use bound parameters (.bind() with $1/? placeholders) or the query!/query_as! macros. Only format! in trusted, non-user-controlled fragments, and confirm it.
